summary: Retire the `manifest` noun (= the committed lock) from doc comments + read-verb diagnostics, and refresh the stale genre/read/drift/adapter section-title citations in the same files

per.section: Naming consistency — the one hard rule

edit.description: Doc comments (99, 486, 587, 616, 756, 781, 973, 1000, 1766) and the two user-facing leaf-not-found diagnostics (639, 800: `"...off the manifest's [[member.genre]] tables..."`) name the committed lock as `the manifest` — a retired noun (05-model.md dissolved list; 90-spec-system migration trail: manifest retired, the lock is the committed anchor). Rename to `the lock`. Diagnostics must speak the corpus vocabulary (50-distribution, no synonyms). Also refresh the stale section-title citations: `20-surface.md`, "The member document" → "The member"; "Genre values" → the current genre section; "Decision: the CLI gains a read family" and "The family gains leaf grain" → "Decision: one read verb — `explain`". Rerun `cargo insta accept` if the reworded diagnostics move any read_verbs snapshot.

Line 160 doc comment "serialized whole into the manifest" → "into the lock". Refresh its stale citations `15-kinds.md`, "genres (optional)" and `20-surface.md`, "Genre values — prose that declares its own anatomy" → `15-kinds.md`, "A genre is a kind at the block locus". Also refresh the stale "A built-in kind is an adapter" / "the adapter faces are declared" citations to current framing (15-kinds.md: built-ins are declared SDK values in a published module, not per-kind adapters). LEAVE the `genre.manifest` fence-info-string test fixtures (988, 1000) — arbitrary fenced-block labels, not the retired noun.

Line 9 doc comment "the manifest's TOML syntax" → "the lock's TOML syntax". Refresh the stale citation `20-surface.md`, "The display rule owns connective tissue" to the current emit/display-rule section.

Line 194 doc comment "the manifest serializer" → "the lock serializer". Refresh stale citations ("Genre values", "`impact` reports at leaf grain", "leaf addresses are structural and keyed") against current headings. LEAVE the `genre.manifest` fence-info fixtures (967, 977).

Line 559 comment "Never gated on a manifest's presence" → "a lock's presence". Refresh stale citations "Drift — two freshness facts" → "The lock and drift — one vocabulary"; "Genre values"; "The member document" → "The member".

Line 774 comment cites `20-surface.md`, "`config.stale` — the committed manifest/projection does not match..." (noun + title both stale) and line 770 "Drift — one direction, two freshness facts" → both to "The lock and drift — one vocabulary"; rename `manifest/projection` → `lock/projection`. Verify the inline paraphrase "a kind's runtime residue is its five declaration facts" still resolves.

Blast radius only: if the reworded leaf-not-found diagnostics (read.rs 639/800) are exercised, regenerate the affected `tests/snapshots/read_verbs__*.snap` via `cargo insta accept`. No new assertions.

acceptance: No live `manifest` noun naming the committed lock survives in src/read.rs, kind.rs, display.rs, extract.rs, main.rs, drift.rs (doc comments and read-verb diagnostics say `lock`); the only surviving `manifest` uses are real plugin/marketplace/hooks/package.json JSON manifests, the compose.rs 'manifest era' tombstone, and `genre.manifest` test fixtures; every spec-section-title citation in those six files resolves to a current `specs/` heading; `cargo test` green.
notes: `manifest` retired 2026-07-04 (05-model.md dissolved list; the lock is the committed anchor). Live residue: read.rs (~10 sites), kind.rs:160, display.rs:9, extract.rs:194, main.rs:559, drift.rs:774. KEEP: real plugin/marketplace/hooks + package.json JSON manifests, the compose.rs 'manifest era' tombstone, and `genre.manifest` fixture info-strings. read.rs:639/800 are user-facing diagnostics — `cargo insta accept` if snapshots move.

summary: Refresh stale spec-section-title citations in engine-side doc comments (predicate algebra, node-set scope, Reporters, retired apply three-states) against current corpus headings — comment-only

per.section: Naming consistency — the one hard rule

edit.description: Refresh cited titles: "The primitive algebra" → "The predicate algebra — closed, decidable, every member decides"; verify "Judged at the node-set scope" resolves (10-contracts.md uses it inline, not as a `##` heading).
"The primitive algebra" → "The predicate algebra". The "reject min>max" hit is an inline value quote, not a section title — leave it.

"The set scope" / "The set scope (the roster)" → "The node-set scope — predicates over a matched set" (45-governance.md); "Decision: the contract is itself checked — admissibility" → "Admissibility — the contract is itself checked, one stage" (10-contracts.md). LEAVE the arbitrary test kind-name "manifest" fixture.
"Outward seams — Reporters" → "Reporters — one diagnostic source, three targets" (50-distribution.md).

"Drift / apply — three states" → retired (20-surface.md "Decision: drift routes to the authored source — no reverse parse": the three-state merge and `re-add`/`apply` are gone). Reconcile the comment to the current disk-vs-lock drift model.
"A built-in kind is an adapter" → current framing (15-kinds.md: built-ins are declared SDK values in a published module, not per-kind adapters). Refresh to the live section/framing.
"The extraction algebra" cites an inline concept, not a heading — reconcile to 05-model.md "The spine: two algebras, two walls" or the 15-kinds extraction section, whichever the comment means.
"Requirements and `satisfies`" → the current owning heading (10-contracts.md "Requirements — the harness's named obligations" or 45-governance.md node-set scope). Verify and refresh.
"The harness is a graph too" → current 45-governance.md "One graph" (or the live heading the comment means). Verify it is a title citation, not inline prose.

acceptance: Every `specs/.../*.md`- or `.claude/rules/*.md`-quoted section title in the touched engine-side files resolves to a heading that currently exists in the corpus; comment-only, no behavior change; `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Candidate stale titles (verify each vs specs/ + .claude/rules/; leave paraphrases that still resolve): 'The primitive algebra'→'The predicate algebra'; 'The set scope'→'The node-set scope'; 'Outward seams — Reporters'→'Reporters — one diagnostic source, three targets'; 'Drift/apply — three states'→retired. Comment-only. kind.rs/check-adapter citations belong to RETIRE-MANIFEST-NOUN (kind.rs) — disjoint. install.rs excluded (its `^0.0.2` SDK pin is release-owned, PACKAGING-CHANNELS).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
